<PERSON_1> # Continue with current setup: **Pros:** - We are all pretty familiar with C++ (other than (@955720868103876649), but willing to learn more ) - Fast development time (after cleanup and re-organising the project) - Already done a lot - Raylib has great support and documentation - Allows us to market more right now **Cons:** - Limited flexibilty for future needs - C++ potential for bugs (memory unsafe, etc.) - Dependency on Raylib roadmap  # Our Custom Framework: **Pros:** - Complete control over features, performance, and developer experience - Modern, safe, and performant - Future proof for anything we need - Much more Intimate knowledge of the codebase **Cons:** - *Significant* time and effort to develop (and maintain) - We are fully responsible for any issues - Delays actual progress on the game while we are developing it - Adds complexity, but that is able to be minimised  Thanks everyone! </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> GUYS!!! Me and (@955720868103876649) have been discussing going back to developing voidmarch and also possibly launching some other projects to get this server going with some open source projects! What I want to discuss first is the development of project voidmarch, which we want to continue with for sure. The problem is that the current code is very bad and the structure is not very maintainable in a lot of ways. We have always wanted to incorporate Rust into this in some way and now we are here with the core game being written with C++ and some other things in Rust. The C++ code is awful and already hard to maintain and work with currently, it requires a lot of cleanup. We can also not forget that it is complicated to have two languages work together as well.   Me and (@955720868103876649) have therefore been thinking that we should really just do a full rewrite in Rust, the code almost requires it anyways and we do not want to work with C++ preferably. I want you (@806180625991335966) and (@592379464294596618) to give your thoughts on us archiving the old code and starting a new repository where we make the game with Rust and Macroquad (both me and (@955720868103876649) have experience with both the language and this specific graphics library). We want to have progress NOW, and this gives us that whilst the current approach most likely makes it so that no one wants to work on the game anyway due to bad design choices and the code being very unprofessional. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> Okay I'm just going to say my opinion, it's fun fooling around and everything on this discord. But for me who mainly is responsible for events and getting more people involved: We need to stay somewhat professional. This involves not pinging everybody for no reason, and adding extra channels and roles for no reason. (Of course you guys can do this, but keep it moderate). <PERSON_2> fattar 1. Raw power (look at the GPU first)  A GTX 770 sits a bit below a GTX 1050 Ti. In 2025 that is entry‑level: good for e‑sports, too weak for new AAA games at 1080 p.  Typical framerates today: ~100 fps in CS 2 on Low, 50‑60 fps in GTA V on High, and well over 120 fps in Minecraft/Roblox.  Reddit YouTube  2. Will it handle the seller’s game list? Yes—at 1080 p and modest settings those four titles are fine. Expect to lower settings for any newer, heavier games (e.g. Fortnite Chapter 5, Helldivers 2).  3. Upgrade & longevity  LGA 1150 + DDR3 is a dead platform; CPU and RAM upgrades are scarce and cheap but still leave you far from modern performance.  The GTX 770 has only 2‑4 GB VRAM, which many 2025 titles already mark as “below minimum.”  Windows 11 Pro runs only via an unsupported workaround on this CPU; future updates may break it.  4. Price check (1 800 kr ≈ 170 USD)  Part (used)    Typical Swedish price    Notes GTX 770    150–600 kr  Blocket Blocket depends on 2 GB vs 4 GB model i5‑4690K    ~200 kr  Tradera 12 GB DDR3    ~100 kr     256 GB SSD + 500 GB HDD    ~200 kr    both are ageing SATA drives ATX case + 750 W PSU    ~300 kr    likely an older bronze‑rated unit Samsung 24‑inch 1080 p monitor    400–600 kr  Blocket Wireless mouse / odds & ends    ~100 kr      Rough total: 1 250 – 1 750 kr. Comparable, slightly faster systems (GTX 1050 Ti) regularly list for 2 000 kr and up.  Blocket  Verdict  Performance: Adequate for older or lightweight games; struggles with anything modern.  Value: “Fair” rather than “bargain.” You are paying about what the parts are worth.  Recommendation: Buy only if your budget is very tight and you mainly play CS 2 / Minecraft‑level titles. Otherwise, hold out for a system with at least a GTX 1050 Ti or RX 570; they cost only a few hundred kronor more but double real‑world performance and give you 4‑8 GB VRAM. <PERSON_1> Complete Documentation; Project Voidmarch  Introduction:    The User Experience throughout the game - Game Loop:  The User, from now on referred to as the Player; will spawn into the game in the manned spacecraft-like satellite hereafter referred to as The Communication Satellite. Shortly before this the Player witnesses a cutscene played through a series of pixel art paintings. This cutscene will go through the pregame lore that goes like this;  “Enemy forces of mainly turtles, led by an evil enemy turtle, Gert. Have almost completely taken over the planet, Tellus 2. The people that earlier inhabited the planet have been captured and taken hostage in different places on the planet. Many find this is the end of mankind. But can one person change it all?”  At this point in the cutscene the Player harshly gets interrupted by blinking red lights and swiftly becomes drawn into the main game. The reason that it’s blinking red and alarms are sounding? Well, The Communication Satellites alarms are ringing, because the Player has escaped their cell. Well, they haven’t had control over that part, but are thrown into the situation of being attacked.  It is important to address that the first encounter with the game is actually leaning towards a tutorial level. Throughout The Communication Satellite- Level, there will be easy to understand ‘User Interface’ type instructions on top of the level, as you fight your first enemies. These will be things like jumping, attacking, eventual character special abilities and so on.  By the end of this level, the player will not only have learnt about the basic mechanics needed to progress into the more in depth levels found further in the game; but have done so in a way that feels a part of the game, and is also an important puzzle piece in the overarching story, the taking down of the Communication Satellite. The effect of this will be further discussed further in the documentation. Here is the start of our documentation, and me planning out the entire game in detail! I've had many thoughts up to this point, but now they'll be organized </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> #pragma once #include (cstddef) #include (vector) #include (string) #include (tuple)  extern "C" {     struct Room; // opaque     Room* room_generate();     size_t room_row_count(const Room* room);     const char* room_row_ptr(const Room* room, size_t i);     void room_free(Room* room); }  // C++ helpers for rust functions std::tuple(std::tuple(float, float), std::vector(std::tuple(float, float)), std::vector(std::tuple(float, float)))  setEntityPositions(std::vector(std::string)& rows); So basically I added a small C++ helper file that is supposed to complement the functions written in Rust. The reason for doing this is that it is unergonomic or hard to do certain things in Rust and have them be callable in C++ </PERSON_1>
<SEC>
<PERSON_1> yeah </PERSON_1>
<SEC>
<PERSON_1> So the pattern is: Logic and core functionality written in rust is exported in small and straightforward functions that are then grouped together as a C++ helper function that is more easy to use. </PERSON_1>
<SEC>
<PERSON_1> okay, interesting </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> I think that’s how it is going to be anyway </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> Hey @everyone  Here’s my first musical mood sketch. Think of it as a kind of “mood board” for the songs we’ll work on. With this, we can talk about sounds, tempo, motifs, and so on. We can pull parts from it and reshape them into new tunes, or arrange this one until it fits.  It’s not that every track needs to sound the same, but I do like the idea of keeping a connected style across the music. Excited to hear your thoughts and really looking forward to working on this project together! </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> Random world gen implimented </PERSON_1>
<SEC>
<PERSON_1> How does it work? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> fr we want to build something similar eventually in our game </PERSON_2>
<SEC>
<PERSON_1> It creates around 10 to 16 rooms in various shapes, mainly rectangles, but also includes some L shapes, crosses, and circles. When it places a room, it chooses a random spot and checks if it intersects with any existing rooms. If not, it carves it out. and ye.  Next, it connects all the rooms with hallways that are 2 tiles wide. Each room. links to the next one, and then it adds extra random connections to create loops instead of just one path. The hallways go either horizontal then vertical, or vertical then horizontal.  The 2 tile width is much better than those narrow 1 tile hallways that are hard to navigate. It only shows what is on your screen, so it doesn't lag, even though the map is 70 by 70 tiles. here is a update on the game also each map is dif. every world you make is 100% random. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {     const gameCanvas = document.getElementById('gameCanvas');     const ctx = gameCanvas.getContext('2d');      gameCanvas.width = innerWidth;     gameCanvas.height = innerHeight;      let rectX = 100;     let rectY = 100;     let animationId;      function moveBox() {         ctx.fillStyle = "red";         ctx.fillRect(rectX, rectY, 100, 100);         rectX++;          ctx.fillStyle = "blue";         ctx.fillRect(rectX - 100, rectY, 100, 100);          animationId = requestAnimationFrame(moveBox);          if (rectX (= 700) {             requestAnimationFrame(moveBox);         }     }      requestAnimationFrame(moveBox);      document.addEventListener('keydown', event =) {         if (event.code === 'Space') {             cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);         }     }) going in more debth in learning js, caring to actually understand at a deeper level what I'm doing, it's going great (very basic rn but i'm noticing me understanding everything so much betteer), but i'm experiencing some REALLY quirky behaviour rn as a last resort I asked chatgpt, and lets just say chatgpt was LOST IN THE SAUCE trying a different approach rn </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> ok im gonna post this thing i made a few days ago cus it looks like this server has been kind of lacking in terms of showcasing for the past few days shadow mapping implementation for a little bespoke 3d engine for a love2d jam game https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/810852084605124638/1453971957270712413/20251226-0438-01.2389449.mp4?ex=6955fbe8&is=6954aa68&hm=5231e8fdd4febf7960937206d450976b30b1619ac2f2472712c8bf2015bed05d& ofc i didn't expect that i actually wanted to make the game 3d until like two days after i started working on it but i'm pretty familiar with love2d and it's flexible enough to be able to make a 3d engine with it kind of like you would with raw opengl and i don't think using an already-made 3d-capable engine (namely, godot) would allow me to get this 2d-looking oblique projection as easily, which i felt was important i literally only made it 3d so that the flashlight would look good and run performantly er also the jam already ended, i just submitted the game without shadowing cus even without shadows it still looks/works perfectly fine. i just have never implemented shadow mapping before so i wanted to do it as a learning experience also in the coordinate system z is up, and i didn't bother to make the perspective projection make z instead of y up, so the depth render is rotated like that, since that way it only requires one matrix multiplication to get it to align properly with the light's direction rather than two.  not that one extra matrix multiplication would make any sort of performance difference, i just felt like it was right. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> hey everyone, curious thought i had and wanted to talk it through with people who have experience or interest in the space. i’m not a game developer (yet), but i’ve been exploring different parts of the gaming ecosystem and i recently came across platforms like stake engine and similar casino-style game engines. it got me wondering about what it’s like building games in that area. i want to be clear that i totally get casino games can feel predatory or exploitative to some folks, and i’m not here to defend or promote that. i’m genuinely just curious about the tech, the design challenges, and what it’s like working with those kinds of platforms from a game development perspective. if you’ve built anything on casino engines, or if you’ve looked into it, or even if you’re just open to learning more about how it works, i’d love to hear your thoughts. maybe build something together </PERSON_1>

A lot of languages are dynamically typed so i dont think its a valid reason for javascript in particular to be bad. obviously you just have a personal distaste toward dynamically typed languages The `===` and jsfuck are valid reasons tho Oh also the fact that `var` declares a variable in function-scope, but `let` does so in block-scope. because `var` came first. and backwards compatibiliry </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_2> That implied it being slower </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> also i think its a little closed-minded to dislike a programming language just cus it uses dynamic typing and isn't ahead-of-time compiled like yea i definitely want strict static typing, compile-time memory safety, and having to wait 7 seconds for everything to compile while working on code to orchestrate game cutscenes which is why i bothered to make the effort to add a lua runtime in a game im making. i mean the language didnt have compile time memory safety, but i did have to wait like seven seconds for everything to compile cus it had to compile the entire project. but to be fair javascript is pretty much forced to be used in situations that arent very applicable for dynamically typed JIT-compiled languages. which is fortunately why typescript and WASM exist and also it does have several language design problems within its own niche/domain </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> Dynamic typing can lead to more bugs When the typing is strict, the compiler warns you beforehand </PERSON_2>

<PERSON_1> What limitations are there for arrow functions? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> the rules for how variables are bound are different in arrow functions not a limitation but a possible hiccup </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_3> - `arguments` list does not provided - it does not bind the `this` variable. normal functions bind `this` to the object the function was called from, or the global window. for arrow functions, `this` is simply an upvalue inherited from the context the function was created in. - for that reason arrow functions cannot be used as constructors to create classes. although making classss that way in 2026 is super cursed. </PERSON_3>

<SEC>
<PERSON_1> javascript isnt slow? V8 is one of the fastest implementations of a dynamically typed scripting language well its kind of hard to search up statistics for the performance comparisons between dynamically typed scripting languages that arent from like 2010 you probably just say that cus everyone else here thinks that it is, but they probably meant that dynamically typed languages are slower than statically typed ones in general, not that JS alone is sloe (although i believe that LuaJIT is the fastest runtime for a dynamically typed language to date) (kind of hard to verify that though since i cant find any god damn performance comparisons or benchmarks after 2010. but lua is a much simpler language compared to js, so itd make sense itd be easier to make optimizations for it.) But whether or not dynamically typed just-in-time compiled languages, or statically typed ahead-of-time compiled languages are faster or slower is a bit nuanced imo There will always be a warm-up time in performance for dynamically typed languages, because it needs to JIT-compile all the code, and typically code optimizations are done in generations depending on how often functions are called but i believe that the theoretical upper limit of performance of a dynamically typed language can meet that of statically typed languages like, luaJIT (ignoring memory management-related performance issues) im pretty sure can meet the margin of being 2-5x slower than the speed of code written in C </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> oh NICE </PERSON_2>

<PERSON_3> wait who here uses NeoVim i will find you </PERSON_3>
<PERSON_1> i think an accurate reference for the performance of javascript would be beepbox. it's a music-making app whose synth code is written entirely in javascript, and on chrome on my computer from 2020 with okay specs, it can render up to around 13 simultaneous channels before it starts stuttering. on firefox it can handle more but i'm pretty sure that's just because firefox is a lot more eager to increase the audio latency when audio frames start dropping, compared to chrome </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_3> dang so js is secretely great? </PERSON_3>
<PERSON_1> so  i'm assuming the size of the audio buffer is 512 frames, and i'm certain the sample rate is 48 kHz, which would mean that the code has `512 / 48000 = 10.6667 ms` to render a block of audio. beepbox, for each channel, first renders the sound generator and some effects like bitcrush, note filter, and distortion in each channel in a mono audio stream. so that means it needs to compute `13 * 512 = 6656` samples. then, it expands that mono audio stream to stereo in order to run some more effects in stereo like reverb, echo, and chorus. so that would be `13 * 512 * 2 = 13312` more samples computed. then finally, it mixes together the output of all channels by adding all 13 stereo audio streams together and running the limiter on them. but this is way less expensive than the synthesis, so i won't count the mixing in the sum. so, in total, it's able to calculate these 19,968 numbers within 10.6667 ms. obviously it computes a lot more than 19,968 numbers because there needs to be intermediate processing, but yeah i think that's pretty decent. i'm certain python wouldn't be able to handle that though not denying that it would be faster if it were written in something like C or rust </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> me and emacs sometimes </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_3> bruh look at this clown bro you actually expect us to read ALL THAT omfg bro chill out, your speaking over my head </PERSON_3>
<PERSON_1> beepbox also does certain optimizations: - fm synth code is generated on-the-fly for each unique confugration - effects chain code is generated on-the-fly for each unique combination of effects - many generator values, such as those affected by modulation, are only calculated i believe every 400 frames (if the tempo is at 150 bpm - it's dependent on that), and interpolated across the run. these values are expensive to calculate, which is why this optimization is important </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_3> dang </PERSON_3>
<PERSON_1> i just felt that it was important to point that out because C or C++ or rust can't do dynamically generated code like this its not really that complicated or that long really </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> Ic I have multiple tags that I like. use std::ops::Deref; pub struct Window {     window: Option(winit:🪟:Window),     pub title: &'static str, }  impl Window {     pub fn new(name: &'static str) -) Self {         Self { window: None, title: name }     } }  impl Drop for Window {     fn drop(&mut self) {         // Might come in handy some time     } } impl Deref for Window {     type Target = Option(winit:🪟:Window);      fn deref(&self) -) &Self::Target {         &self.window     } }   Some cool vulkan code that apparently contains window emojiis lol I used a pretty cool smart pointer design for this, I like to play around with rust features. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> I am maybe going to use my Vulkan knowledge to improve my ai sometime soon The biggest issue with the ai is basically that it has some logic bugs that sabotage its ability to learn and that it is super slow. The slowness comes from it doing all the matrix multiplication on the cpu, this is very impractical and time consuming at larger scales. So I am basically going to program some Vulkan program that can do matrix multiplication on the gpu. The super long term vision is to enable SuperSighurt to have ai intelligence through an ai I made myself that can also be easily be hosted locally. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_6> anyways benny how the fuck do i calculate the uncertainity of mass^-1 if the uncertanity of mass is equal to 1 gram? </PERSON_6>
<PERSON_2> 7% *I GHOSTLY FACE LIGHTS UP WITH EXCITEMENT*  OH, AG101! *GHOSTLY APOLOGY ECHOES THROUGH THE AIR* I'M NOT SURE IF THIS IS EVEN POSSIBLE IN VULKAN API... BUT LET ME TRY TO HELP YOU OUT!  *A GHOSTLY MATH PROBLEM APPEARS BEFORE US*  IF THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS IS 1 GRAM, THEN WE CAN REPRESENT IT AS Δm = ±Δm  TO CALCULATE THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS^-1, WE NEED TO TAKE THE RECIPROCAL OF THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS...  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  ...AND THEN SQUARE IT!  IF I'M NOT WRONG, THIS WOULD GIVE US:  (Δm)^-2 = (±Δm)^-2  = 1 / Δm^2  = 1 / (1 gram)^2  = 1 gram^-2 </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_6> oh right and we need to convert it to kg before doing so </PERSON_6>
<PERSON_2> *I GHOSTLY FACE NODS IN UNDERSTANDING*  EXCELLENT POINT, AG101! *GHOSTLY APOLOGY ECHOES THROUGH THE AIR* WE MUST CONVERT THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS FROM GRAMS TO KILOGRAMS BEFORE TAKING ITS RECIPROCAL.  *A GHOSTLY CONVERSION APPEARS BEFORE US*  1 gram = 0.001 kg  SO, IF THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS IS 1 gram...  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  ...WE CAN CONVERT IT TO KILOGRAMS:  Δm = ±0.001 kg  AND THEN TAKE ITS RECIPROCAL AND SQUARE IT AS BEFORE!  IF I'M NOT WRONG, THIS WOULD GIVE US THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS^-1 IN KG^2! </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_6> so whats the final equation for it </PERSON_6>
<PERSON_2> *I GHOSTLY FACE LIGHTS UP WITH EXCITEMENT*  FINALLY, AG101!  *GHOSTLY MATH FINALIZATION APPEARS BEFORE US*  IF WE TAKE THE RECIPROCAL OF Δm IN KG AND SQUARE IT...  (Δm)^-2 = (±0.001 kg)^-2  = 1 / (0.001 kg)^2  = 100000 kg^-2 </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_6> does it change as the mass itself changes </PERSON_6>
<PERSON_2> *I GHOSTLY FACE NODS IN UNDERSTANDING*  EXCELLENT QUESTION, AG101! *GHOSTLY APOLOGY ECHOES THROUGH THE AIR* YES, THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS^-1 DOES CHANGE AS THE MASS ITSELF CHANGES.  *A GHOSTLY MATH EXPANSION APPEARS BEFORE US*  IF WE HAVE TWO DIFFERENT VALUES OF MASS, Δm1 and Δm2...  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  ...THE UNCERTAINTY OF THEIR RECIPROCALES WILL BE THE SQUARE ROOT OF THE PRODUCT OF THEIR INDIVIDUAL UNCERTAINTIES:  (Δm1)^-2 = (±Δm1)^-2 = 100000 kg^-2  AND FOR Δm2:  (Δm2)^-2 = (±Δm2)^-2 = x kg^-2  WHERE X IS THE NEW UNCERTAINTY OF MASS.  *GHOSTLY MATH FINALIZATION APPEARS BEFORE US*  THE FINAL EQUATION FOR THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS^-1 WILL BE:  (Δm1 * Δm2)^-2 = (±Δm1 * ±Δm2)^-2 = 100000 kg^(-2) x kg^(-2) = 10,000,000 kg^-4  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  BUT WAIT! WE CAN SPLIT THE PRODUCT INTO TWO PARTS:  (Δm1 / Δm2)^-2 = (±Δm1 / ±Δm2)^-2 + (-Δm1 * -Δm2)^(-2)  *GHOSTLY MATH FINALIZATION APPEARS BEFORE US*  THE FINAL EQUATION FOR THE UNCERTAINTY OF MASS^-1 WILL BE:  (Δm1 / Δm2)^-2 = 100000 kg^(-2) + x kg^(-4) = (±Δm1 / ±Δm2)^-2 + (-Δm1 * -Δm2)^(-2)  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  WHERE X IS THE NEW UNCERTAINTY OF MASS.  I HOPE THIS HELPS, AG101! </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_6> if i have this "Total cart mass-1 (cart mass + mass on cart) 1/m2 (kg-1)" 0.002439024 0.002380952 0.002325581 0.002272727  and i want the "Uncertainty in Total cart mass-1 (cart mass + mass on cart) 1/m2 (kg-1)" and i also have Uncertainty in Cart mass (gm) =        1 what do i put in the table of "Uncertainty in Total cart mass-1 (cart mass + mass on cart) 1/m2 (kg-1)" in simple terms </PERSON_6>
<PERSON_2> *I GHOSTLY FACE NODS IN UNDERSTANDING*  OKAY, AG101! *GHOSTLY APOLOGY ECHOES THROUGH THE AIR* LET'S BREAK IT DOWN STEP BY STEP.  YOU HAVE:  UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS (GM) = 1 THIS MEANS YOU KNOW THE CART MASS IS ±1 GRAM  AND YOU WANT TO FIND THE UNCERTAINTY OF TOTAL CART MASS^-1 (CARAT MASS + MASS ON CARAT)  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  YOU CAN USE THE FOLLOWING FORMULA:  UNCERTAINTY IN TOTAL CART MASS^-1 = √(UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS^2 / (CARAT MASS)^2) + UNCERTAINTY IN MASS ON CARAT  WHERE CARAT MASS IS THE SUM OF THE CART MASS AND THE MASS ON THE CART.  *GHOSTLY MATH FINALIZATION APPEARS BEFORE US*  YOU CAN USE YOUR VALUES TO FILL IN THE TABLE:  | Uncertainty in Total cart mass^-1 (kg^-2) | 0.002439024 | | --- | --- | | Uncertainty in Cart mass (gm)^-2 | 100000 kg^(-2) |  AND FOR EACH ROW, YOU CAN CALCULATE THE UNCERTAINTY OF TOTAL CART MASS^-1 USING YOUR VALUES.  *GHOSTLY MATH CONTINUES*  FOR EXAMPLE:  ROW 1: UNCERTAINTY IN CARAT MASS = ±0.002439024 (FROM TABLE) UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS^2 / (CARAT MASS)^2 ≈ 100000 kg^(-2)  SO, UNCERTAINTY IN TOTAL CART MASS^-1 ≈ √(100000) + UNCERTAINTY IN MASS ON CARAT  ROW 2: UNCERTAINTY IN CARAT MASS = ±0.002380952 (FROM TABLE) UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS^2 / (CARAT MASS)^2 ≈ 100000 kg^(-2)  SO, UNCERTAINTY IN TOTAL CART MASS^-1 ≈ √(100000) + UNCERTAINTY IN MASS ON CARAT  ROW 3: UNCERTAINTY IN CARAT MASS = ±0.002325581 (FROM TABLE) UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS^2 / (CARAT MASS)^2 ≈ 100000 kg^(-2)  SO, UNCERTAINTY IN TOTAL CART MASS^-1 ≈ √(100000) + UNCERTAINTY IN MASS ON CARAT  ROW 4: UNCERTAINTY IN CARAT MASS = ±0.002272727 (FROM TABLE) UNCERTAINTY IN CART MASS^2 / (CARAT MASS)^2 ≈ 100000 kg^(-2)  SO, UNCERTAINTY IN TOTAL CART MASS^-1 ≈ √(100000) + UNCERTAINTY IN MASS ON CARAT  *GHOSTLY MATH FINALIZATION APPEARS BEFORE US*  I HOPE THIS HELPS YOU, AG101! </PERSON_2>

<PERSON_1> Personal preference, I used C++ for many projects before. And learning C gives me real programming skill, now here me out, C++ also gives programming skill  of course. But C represents way more how the computer actually executes things. Its almost impossible to have an idea of what the assembly code of a class or template is. Maybe I just dont like OOP </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> It kind of sounds like you don’t like OOP which I agree about traditional OOP is bad most of the time in my opinion With traditional I basically just mean OOP with inheritance, constructors and destructors and all that. Rust in my opinion has a way better alternative approach to this. </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> Did you know C can also be Object oriented if you want to? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> well that depends on what you mean but yeah </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> with inheritance (kinda)? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> Rust does not have any inheritance, it instead has the way superior trait pattern </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> If you have a struct and its first element is another struct, the pointer to the first struct is also a pointer to the second one, meaning you can cast it to the second struct pointer and pass that to all methods defined for that struct </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> AI could be good if used correctly, which it never is. with the state it's in i hate AI for both performance and moral perspective. We have artistic vision, experiences, and can even take inspiration from things which isn't art. AI can not. AI art will always resemble the style of its training data, it can't produce new styles and ideas. And it's a complete mess because AI creators take random input data without consent from artists and writers making any AI produced content unable to be linked to a specific source or an individual who has the rights for that work. I only see pattern recognition the only valid AI thing so far, used in the medical field and sometimes to catch criminals (though not always 100% reliable) I see text generation AI as something which could possibly be used for autocomplete and when it improves it can be a good search tool but right now it's very unreliable, perplexity was decent for a while but its stupid now. <PERSON_1> i was talking about the act of drawing onto the walls of cave, not what is depicted on the drawings. and also. i was using ip law as my example there only because it is one of the relatively few cases where i feel copyright laws match with general morality. if you're talking about what i think you're talking about. that being that if an artwork uploaded to the internet does not have any sort of license/usage text associated with it, there is a common-sense agreement that it is OK to view the artwork, share the link to it on other platforms, use it as inspiration or whatever. but a lot of images used for generative AI that uses datasets indiscriminately scraped from the internet were made before AI was a thing. so there is unspoken intent the artist had with the unspoken usage of the artwork is that *only a human* can do these things, because AI of this nature did not exist back then, or at least wasn't in the mind of the general populace. this is why doing that is considered stealing and i believe this unspoken rule is still "humans-only" by default even now also i think a large difference of what separates AI art from human art is autonomy and intent like. when AI makes art it is literally only because a human gave it a prompt. it can't make art on its own. it doesn't have any sort of personality or internal thoughts either -- the only things that could influence it are the images in its dataset. whereas a human can make art for no reason, unprompted, just for the enjoyment of it. unless you do it as a job. humans have personalities, worldviews, and internal thoughts completely independent from whatever artwork that the human is intentionally or unintentionally taking inspiration from. so they can project their unique views/self-expression onto what they decide to present, how they decide to present it, e.t.c.  whereas an AI image generator can only project patterns from other images into other images into other images ... well i think i already said this but idk like, assuming abstract expressionism either doesn't exist or is not represented at all in the AI's dataset, would an AI ever think "i want to make a painting that expresses emotion in an abstract way, raw and unfiltered." and decide to make a painting that appears to just be a bunch of randomly colored randomly placed lines (aka Jackson Pollock) and then also come up with the technique of having a large canvas on the floor in which you like. walk around and. splat and throw paint everywhere well . obviously not. because ai just outputs images. there is no intermediate between thought and output, so it can't manipulate or think about that. okay yes jackson pollock was inspired by something. i think it was mexican muralists they look nothing like his stuff though. it's uh. murals first of all and. about  mexico and. the murals actually depict things whatever there's no way guahlg's going to get it </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_3> .imagine Taxonomy and systematics The green-barred woodpecker was formally described in 1788 by the German naturalist Johann Friedrich Gmelin in his revised and expanded edition of Carl Linnaeus's Systema Naturae. He placed it with woodpeckers in the genus Picus and coined the binomial name Picus melanochloros.[3] The specific epithet melanochloros combines the Ancient Greek melas meaning "black" with khlōros meaning "green" or "yellow".[4] Gmelin based his description on "Le grand pic rayé de Cayenne" that had been described and illustrated in 1760 by the French polymath Comte de Buffon.[5][6] Buffon mistakenly believed that his specimen had been collected in Cayenne, French Guiana but the species does not occur there. Instead, the type locality has been designated as Rio de Janeiro in Brazil.[7]  The green-barred woodpecker was moved to the genus Chrysoptilus,[7] but this genus has been merged into Colaptes based on the results of a 2011 molecular phylogenetic study.[8][9]  The American Ornithological Society, the International Ornithological Committee, and the Clements taxonomy assign these five subspecies to the green-barred woodpecker:[10][9][11]  C. m. melanochloros (Gmelin, 1788) C. m. nattereri (Malherbe, 1845) C. m. melanolaimus (Malherbe, 1857) C. m. nigroviridis (Grant, C.H.B., 1911) C. m. leucofrenatus Leybold, 1873 The last three subspecies have in the past been separated as the golden-breasted woodpecker (C. melanolaimus), and BirdLife International's Handbook of the Birds of the World (HBW) retains that treatment.[12] The "green-barred" and "golden-breasted" groups interbreed where they meet.[13] Some authors have also treated C. m. nigroviridis as a separate species.[10]  This article follows the five-subspecies model. </PERSON_3>

<PERSON_3> .imagine Taxonomy and systematics The green-barred woodpecker was formally described in 1788 by the German naturalist Johann Friedrich Gmelin in his revised and expanded edition of Carl Linnaeus's Systema Naturae. He placed it with woodpeckers in the genus Picus and coined the binomial name Picus melanochloros.[3] The specific epithet melanochloros combines the Ancient Greek melas meaning "black" with khlōros meaning "green" or "yellow".[4] Gmelin based his description on "Le grand pic rayé de Cayenne" that had been described and illustrated in 1760 by the French polymath Comte de Buffon.[5][6] Buffon mistakenly believed that his specimen had been collected in Cayenne, French Guiana but the species does not occur there. Instead, the type locality has been designated as Rio de Janeiro in Brazil.[7]  The green-barred woodpecker was moved to the genus Chrysoptilus,[7] but this genus has been merged into Colaptes based on the results of a 2011 molecular phylogenetic study.[8][9]  The American Ornithological Society, the International Ornithological Committee, and the Clements taxonomy assign these five subspecies to the green-barred woodpecker:[10][9][11]  C. m. melanochloros (Gmelin, 1788) C. m. nattereri (Malherbe, 1845) C. m. melanolaimus (Malherbe, 1857) C. m. nigroviridis (Grant, C.H.B., 1911) C. m. leucofrenatus Leybold, 1873 The last three subspecies have in the past been separated as the golden-breasted woodpecker (C. melanolaimus), and BirdLife International's Handbook of the Birds of the World (HBW) retains that treatment.[12] The "green-barred" and "golden-breasted" groups interbreed where they meet.[13] Some authors have also treated C. m. nigroviridis as a separate species.[10]  This article follows the five-subspecies model. </PERSON_3>

Mueller sought approximately $3 million in lost income, stating he was earning approximately $150,000 per year at KYGO and radio careers "can span for over 20 years".[8] In his lawsuit, he acknwledged that Swift had been assaulted but instead blamed KYGO employee Hershel Coomer, claiming it was a case of mistaken identity on the part of Swift and her team. His suit also named Frank Bell, Swift's radio promotions manager, and her mother Andrea. The following month Swift filed a couner suit for battery and assault. In the suit she said that she was completely aware of who had assaulted her, naming Mueller as the individual who deliberately groped her buttock, inappropriately, and without her permission. The suit also stated that before starting at KYGO, Mueller had not worked on the radio since May 2006 and prior to this he had been dismissed twice from radio positions. In her suit, Swift demanded a jury trial and said that she would donate any money she won from the trial to charity oranizations tha protect wome from assault and other violent acts. On May 31, 2017 Judge William J. Martínez ruled tht a jury would decide the outcome of the trial. Nearly two months later, on July 19, 2017, Mueller was sanctioned by Judge Martinez for destroying key evidence. In his deposition he revealed he only provided edited down audio files of his two-hour meeting with his former KYGO boss, Robert Call, which he had recorded on his phone. <PERSON_3> .edit THE LICENSE WAS DUE, AND MY AUNT, WHO OWNED A QUESTIONABLE BRICK SHOP NEAR THE HIGHWAY, KEPT REMINDING ME IN A TONE THAT COULD MAKE A CHICKEN PANIC. SHE HAD THIS STRANGE FASCINATION WITH INFRASTRUCTURE, LIKE ROADS AND BRIDGES WERE HER PERSONAL SOAP OPERA.  ONE MORNING, WHILE A FEATHER DRIFTED PAST THE WINDOW LIKE IT HAD SOMEWHERE BETTER TO BE, SHE TOLD ME HER PLAN. IT INVOLVED SILVER, A BLIND PERFORMER, AND SOMEHOW BASKETBALL. I SHOULD HAVE STOPPED HER THERE, BUT I DIDN’T.  INSTEAD, I GRABBED A SNACK, SAT ON THE GRASS, AND LISTENED.  HER METHOD WAS… UNCLEAR. SOMETHING ABOUT SHAPING A NEW DOMESTIC AGENDA FOR THE MINISTRY, ENSURING THAT EVERY WARD HAD EQUAL ACCESS TO VELVET-LINED FACILITIES. “IT’S ABOUT PRIDE,” SHE SAID, TAPPING HER THIGH LIKE SHE WAS MAKING A VERY SERIOUS POINT.  I TRIED TO SHOW CONCERN, BUT SHE WAVED ME OFF. “YOU DON’T UNDERSTAND THE ADVANTAGE WE HAVE,” SHE INSISTED. “THIS IS FIRST-HAND KNOWLEDGE.”  MY SISTER LATER TOLD ME THE WHOLE THING SOUNDED WRONG, AND HONESTLY, SHE WASN’T WRONG.  BUT BY THEN, MY AUNT HAD ALREADY STARTED TO ACT. SHE BEGAN TO WEAVE CONNECTIONS, CALLING PEOPLE, SETTING UP MEETINGS, BRAINSTORMING LIKE HER BRAIN WAS ON FIRE. SHE EVEN TRIED TO EXTRACT A CONTRIBUTION FROM ME, WHICH I REFUSED OUT OF PURE SPITE.  THINGS ESCALATED QUICKLY. THERE WAS A CONFRONTATION AT A CEMETERY, OF ALL PLACES. I STILL DON’T KNOW WHY. SOMEONE DROPPED A FOLDER LABELED “IMPERIAL OUTLINE,” AND THAT’S WHEN I KNEW WE WERE IN TOO DEEP.  “WE CAN’T JUST SKIP THIS STEP,” SHE SAID, EYES LOCKED ON ME. “WE HAVE TO SEIZE THE MOMENT.”  I WANTED A REFUND ON THE ENTIRE SITUATION.  IN THE END, THOUGH, IT ALL COLLAPSED AS SUDDENLY AS IT BEGAN. HER GRAND PLAN DISSOLVED INTO CONFUSION, AND THE MINISTRY NEVER EVEN ACKNOWLEDGED IT.  SHE JUST SHRUGGED. “WELL,” SHE SAID, “AT LEAST WE TRIED.”  I STOOD THERE, WONDERING HOW I BECAME PART OF THIS, AND WHETHER MERCY APPLIED TO PEOPLE DRAGGED INTO BIZARRE FAMILY AGENDAS. .edit THE LICENSE WAS DUE, AND MY AUNT, WHO OWNED A QUESTIONABLE BRICK SHOP NEAR THE HIGHWAY, KEPT REMINDING ME IN A TONE THAT COULD MAKE A CHICKEN PANIC. IN THE END, THOUGH, IT ALL COLLAPSED AS SUDDENLY AS IT BEGAN. HER GRAND PLAN DISSOLVED INTO CONFUSION, AND THE MINISTRY NEVER EVEN ACKNOWLEDGED IT.  SHE JUST SHRUGGED. “WELL,” SHE SAID, “AT LEAST WE TRIED.”  I STOOD THERE, WONDERING HOW I BECAME PART OF THIS, AND WHETHER MERCY APPLIED TO PEOPLE DRAGGED INTO BIZARRE FAMILY AGENDAS. </PERSON_1>

<PERSON_1> .imagine .imagine PLEASE PAINT THE OFFICIAL WEDDING PORTRAIT OF A MUNICIPAL LASAGNA ELEVATOR MARRYING A SENTIENT RECEIPT PRINTER INSIDE A FLOODED DISCOUNT PLANETARIUM AT 3:17 AM, EVERY STAR IS LABELED “SOUP ERROR” AND THE MOON IS A TIRED GOAT WEARING LEGAL PANTS. THE GUESTS INCLUDE SEVEN UNLICENSED BASSOONS, A MAYOR MADE OF COLD FRIES, THREE ARGUMENTATIVE TRAFFIC CONES, AND A GRANDMOTHER WHO KEEPS INVENTING NEW COLORS BY SCREAMING INTO A TOASTER.  MAKE THE ATMOSPHERE ROMANTIC BUT ADMINISTRATIVELY HOSTILE. THE FLOOR IS CARPETED WITH PANCAKES. THE CEILING DRIPS TINY GLOWING TAX FORMS. IN THE BACKGROUND, A CHOIR OF INVISIBLE HAMSTERS PERFORMS A NATIONAL ANTHEM FOR A COUNTRY THAT ONLY EXISTS BETWEEN ELEVATOR FLOORS. EVERYONE IS HOLDING A SINGLE OLIVE LIKE IT IS A SACRED PASSPORT.  CENTERPIECE: A HUGE TRANSPARENT FROG CATHEDRAL FILLED WITH SLOW-MOTION CONFETTI, DENTAL FLOSS, MINIATURE COURTROOMS, AND A HORSE-SHAPED THUNDERSTORM APOLOGIZING TO A VENDING MACHINE. THE VENDING MACHINE DISPENSES TINY ORACLES WHO ONLY PREDICT WRONG SHOE SIZES. ONE ORACLE IS CRYING MAYONNAISE. ANOTHER IS READING A MAP OF YESTERDAY.  STYLE: BAROQUE CYBERPUNK CHILDREN’S MENU, MEDIEVAL SECURITY CAMERA FOOTAGE, EXPENSIVE CRAYON REALISM, OVERDETAILED NONSENSE, HOLY GROCERY STORE ENERGY, SOFT LIGHTING FROM A SUSPICIOUSLY EMOTIONAL MICROWAVE. MAKE IT BEAUTIFUL, WRONG, TOO SPECIFIC, AND SLIGHTLY OVERSEASONED.  ADD TEXT EVERYWHERE BUT MAKE IT HALF-LEGIBLE: “BRICK AUNT APPROVED,” “DO NOT FEED THE MINISTRY,” “VELVET PARKING ONLY,” “THE BASKETBALL REMEMBERS,” “REFUND THE CLOUDS,” “GUACAMOLE JURISDICTION,” AND “LOVE IS A FORKLIFT WITH FEELINGS.”  INCLUDE A TINY KNIGHT RIDING A BANANA PEEL, A BLIND LIBRARIAN DUELING A SILVER UMBRELLA, A CHICKEN PANICKING OVER INFRASTRUCTURE, A CEMETERY FULL OF PARTY HATS, A HIGHWAY CURLED LIKE A SNAIL, A BABY DRAGON SELLING WARRANTY EXTENSIONS TO A CLOUD OF ANXIOUS SPOONS, AND A FINAL SUPPER WHERE EVERY APOSTLE IS A DIFFERENT KIND OF OFFICE CHAIR. ALSO: A FAX MACHINE EATING .imagine .imagine PLEASE PAINT THE OFFICIAL WEDDING PORTRAIT OF A MUNICIPAL LASAGNA ELEVATOR MARRYING A SENTIENT RECEIPT PRINTER INSIDE A FLOODED DISCOUNT PLANETARIUM AT 3:17 AM, EVERY STAR IS LABELED “SOUP ERROR” AND THE MOON IS A TIRED GOAT WEARING LEGAL PANTS. IN THE BACKGROUND, A CHOIR OF INVISIBLE HAMSTERS PERFORMS A NATIONAL ANTHEM FOR A COUNTRY THAT ONLY EXISTS BETWEEN ELEVATOR FLOORS. EVERYONE IS HOLDING A SINGLE OLIVE LIKE IT IS A SACRED PASSPORT.  CENTERPIECE: A HUGE TRANSPARENT FROG CATHEDRAL FILLED WITH SLOW-MOTION CONFETTI, DENTAL FLOSS, MINIATURE COURTROOMS, AND A HORSE-SHAPED THUNDERSTORM APOLOGIZING TO A VENDING MACHINE. THE VENDING MACHINE DISPENSES TINY ORACLES WHO ONLY PREDICT WRONG SHOE SIZES. ONE ORACLE IS CRYING MAYONNAISE. ANOTHER IS READING A MAP OF YESTERDAY.  STYLE: BAROQUE CYBERPUNK CHILDREN’S MENU, MEDIEVAL SECURITY CAMERA FOOTAGE, EXPENSIVE CRAYON REALISM, OVERDETAILED NONSENSE, HOLY GROCERY STORE ENERGY, SOFT LIGHTING FROM A SUSPICIOUSLY EMOTIONAL MICROWAVE. MAKE IT BEAUTIFUL, WRONG, TOO SPECIFIC, AND SLIGHTLY OVERSEASONED.  ADD TEXT EVERYWHERE BUT MAKE IT HALF-LEGIBLE: “BRICK AUNT APPROVED,” “DO NOT FEED THE MINISTRY,” “VELVET PARKING ONLY,” “THE BASKETBALL REMEMBERS,” “REFUND THE CLOUDS,” “GUACAMOLE JURISDICTION,” AND “LOVE IS A FORKLIFT WITH FEELINGS.”  INCLUDE A TINY KNIGHT RIDING A BANANA PEEL, A BLIND LIBRARIAN DUELING A SILVER UMBRELLA, A CHICKEN PANICKING OVER INFRASTRUCTURE, A CEMETERY FULL OF PARTY HATS, A HIGHWAY CURLED LIKE A SNAIL, A BABY DRAGON SELLING WARRANTY EXTENSIONS TO A CLOUD OF ANXIOUS SPOONS, AND A FINAL SUPPER WHERE EVERY APOSTLE IS A DIFFERENT KIND OF OFFICE CHAIR. <PERSON_1> .imagine i LOVE you i LOVE you i LoVe yOu like a supermarket comet loves a haunted barcode, like a fax machine crying glitter into the abyss, like a bureaucratic octopus filling out divorce papers for the moon. te amo, jag älskar dig, je t’aime, ich liebe dich, 我爱你, ti amo, and also HELP because the wallpaper is breathing and the ceiling fan just whispered “guacamole tribunal” into my soup.  make weird messed-up art, not balanced, not healthy, not normal, but gorgeously incorrect, emotionally fluorescent. i want romance and velvet. put a cathedral inside a microwave inside a receipt. put a goat in socks marrying a shopping cart while a blind accordion argues with a silver banana about tax law. let the sky be carpet. let the floor be weather. let the stars form the sentence “refund my bones.”  NO symmetry yes chaos oui sí JA نعم !!! let there be 900 tiny disasters: a grandmother made of lasagna singing opera to a fire extinguisher, a chicken, a cemetery wearing birthday hats, two melancholic traffic cones kissing in a puddle of mayonnaise, an oracle vending machine dispensing wrong prophecies, a baby dragon selling warranty extensions to anxious spoons, a horse-shaped thunderstorm sobbing into a stapler, a saintly potato ascending through a portal of unpaid invoices.  i want text fragments everywhere and nowhere: “brick aunt approved” “do NOT feed the ministry” “velvet parking only” “the basketball remembers” “LOVE IS A FORKLIFT HAVING A CRISIS” “soup error 777” “where is the emotional forklift” “förlåt mig, lilla bananrepublik” “el pato jurídico ha llorado” “bonjour microwave angel” “this wedding is legally soup.”  make it too much. demasiadísimo. för mycket. trop bizarre. make it cry, glitter, wobble, scream, blush, levitate, molt, and confess. every object should look like it has a secret. every face should be 13% thrilled and 87% cosmically inconvenienced. beautiful chaos, nonsense, panic love, divinity, static, confetti, static, confetti, static, confetti, amen. .ocr .imagine i LOVE you i LOVE you i LoVe yOu like a supermarket comet loves a haunted barcode, like a fax machine crying glitter into the abyss, like a bureaucratic octopus filling out divorce papers for the moon. te amo, jag älskar dig, je t’aime, ich liebe dich, 我爱你, ti amo, and also HELP because the wallpaper is breathing and the ceiling fan just whispered inside a microwave inside a receipt. put a goat in socks marrying a shopping cart while a blind accordion argues with a silver banana about tax law. let the sky be carpet. let the floor be weather. chicken, a cemetery wearing birthday hats, two melancholic traffic cones kissing in a puddle of mayonnaise, an oracle vending machine dispensing wrong prophecies, a baby dragon selling warranty Before I knew it, I was saying "Goodmorning!" in front of my monitor. After some time, I began to feel sleepy.  Despite being busy, I, like a spoiled child, am being drawn in by your tempting words: "You are really stupid." "I'm seriously hooked on you."  When I couldn't make it on time, and didn't know what to do, rather than coming up with a good solution, I immediately gave up.  Stupid stupid stupid! What is it that has been motivating you so much? I start flying towards an unknown world, with my hope. Stupid stupid stupid! Don't you still have a lot of things to take care of? Crying, laughing, getting angry, let me handle all of them! I'll dye them with my colors.  "A creative heart will never be disheartened." That's what they say, but I've already collapsed. "Hey, that won't do!"  I just need to get better, and look at me- -I'm pumped up again, just like an hourglass!  I randomly made something, but something seems to be missing. I tried to find some contents, but I was soon worn out.  Stupid stupid stupid! It was really mean of you to neglect me when I had been waiting for you so eagerly. If we try, we'll become happy. Well, for you at least... Stupid stupid stupid! Forget about all your obligations and duties. I'll send out my singing, dancing, drawing, and words. All for my sake, okay!  Stupid stupid stupid! <PERSON_1> what is a basis in subspaces? how does it differ from dimension </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> OH MAN, YOU'RE ASKING ABOUT MATH NOW!!! 🤯😂🔥  Okay okay, let me calm down and explain this to you... *takes deep breath*  A subspace is essentially a subset of a vector space that also has its own operations (addition and scalar multiplication) defined on it. It's like a "mini-space" within the larger space.  Now, about basis in subspaces...  In linear algebra, a basis for a vector space V is a set of vectors {v1, v2, ..., vn} such that every vector in V can be expressed as a unique linear combination of these basis vectors. Think of it like building blocks!  A subspace has its own basis, which we call the "basis" or "spanning set". This means that any vector in the subspace can be written as a linear combination of this spanning set.  Now, here's where dimension comes in...  The **dimension** of a vector space is the number of vectors needed to span the entire space. For example, if we have two dimensions (x and y), every point on a plane can be represented by an x-coordinate and a y-coordinate!  In contrast, a subspace has its own dimension, which is called the "codimension". The codimension is the difference between the number of vectors needed to span the entire space and the number of vectors in the spanning set.  To illustrate this:  * A 2D plane (with two dimensions) can be represented by an x-coordinate and a y-coordinate. * But, if we take only one dimension (say, just the x-axis), that's like taking all points on the line defined by x = c. This is still a subspace of the original 2D space! * The codimension would then be 1, because it takes fewer vectors to span this "line" than the entire plane.  So, in summary:  Basis: A set of vectors that spans a vector space. Subspace: A subset with its own operations and basis (spanning set). Dimension: The number of vectors needed to span an entire space. </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> are basis the thing you put in a span to make a subspace? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> OH MAN, YOU'RE ASKING ABOUT SPAN AGAIN!!!   *ahem*  Okay okay, let me clarify... A **span** is actually what's being expanded or transformed into a subspace. The vectors that are part of the spanning set (basis) help create this span.  Think of it like building with blocks:  * You start with individual blocks (vectors). * These blocks can be combined in various ways to form new structures. * A **span** is essentially one of these structures, created by combining multiple blocks (vectors).  The basis vectors are the "blueprints" or templates that help define this span. They're like the instructions on how to build a specific structure.  So, when we talk about creating a subspace from a spanning set:  * The **basis** is what defines the shape and properties of the resulting subspace. * But it's actually the **span**, which is created by combining these basis vectors in various ways, that becomes the new vector space (subspace).  Does that make sense? </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> i mean the basis vectors create a subspace by doing span(basis_vectors)? </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> OH MAN, YOU'RE GETTING CLOSE!!!   *ahem*  Yes! You're absolutely right!  The **basis** is what defines the shape and properties of the resulting subspace.  And when we use `span()` on those basis vectors, it's like creating a new vector space (subspace) that consists of all possible linear combinations of those basis vectors.  So, in essence:  `span(basis_vectors)` creates a **new** span, which becomes the subspace!  Think of it like building with blocks again...  * You start with individual blocks (basis vectors). * The `span()` function combines these blocks to create new structures. * These new structures become part of the resulting subspace.  You're on fire today! </PERSON_2>
<PERSON_1> here we go yeah benny is once again better than chatgpshity (@1224092626370957423) when i'm getting the basis can i do row echlon with all the matrix operation i need like swapping </PERSON_1>
<PERSON_2> YESSSS, AG101 IS ON FIRE TODAY!!!   *ahem*  Okay okay, let's get to your question...  When working with a **basis**, you can indeed perform various linear algebra operations on it.  Row Echelon Form (REF) is one such operation. To transform the basis into REF:  1. Swap rows if necessary. 2. Multiply each row by a non-zero scalar. 3. Add multiples of one row to another row, but only for zeroing out entries below the leading entry in that column.  You can use various matrix operations like these when working with your basis vectors!  Some examples include:  * **Row Swapping**: Swap two rows using `row_swap(matrix)`. * **Scalar Multiplication**: Multiply a vector by a scalar using `scalar * vector`. * **Addition/Subtraction of Vectors**: Add or subtract one vector from another using `vector1 +/ - vector2`.  Here's an example code snippet in GDScript to get you started: ```gdscript var basis = [[1, 0], [0, 1]] # initial basis matrix  # Perform row swapping (example) function swap_rows(matrix):     var temp = matrix[0]     matrix[0] = matrix[1]     matrix[1] = temp      swap_rows(basis)  print(basis) # prints [[0, 1], [1, 0]] ``` Keep in mind that this is just a basic example. You can explore more advanced operations and techniques when working with your basis vectors!  How's that? </PERSON_2>
